expectedAssertions: report-quality preserves the host-adapter protection question in JSON and Markdown
the fixture exposes behaviorGates in JSON and Behavior Gates in Markdown
the fixture separates adapter, hardware, requested-explanation, and one-check protection lanes
the fixture keeps scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly explicit
shareable output contains no local absolute paths or private app identifiers
privateDataPolicy: This promoted fixture is synthetic. Use source reports only to choose report shape; do not copy private app code, local paths, screenshots, app-specific identifiers, or proprietary text.

promotion.promotionPolicy: Promotion is explicit maintainer work. ShipGuard writes starter files only to the requested output directory and does not auto-copy them into the repository.
reviewChecklist: Confirm the fixture is synthetic and public-safe.
Confirm no private app code, screenshots, local paths, tokens, app identifiers, or proprietary text were copied.
Confirm behavior gates are visible in both JSON and Markdown.
Confirm scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly remain explicit.
Confirm scoring the promoted fixture does not emit recursive fixtureCandidates.

publicFixtureRecipe: Create a minimal synthetic ShipGuard Lean Deck report pair that proves less-code pressure keeps host adapters, hardware calibration, requested explanations, and one runnable check as protected lanes.
sourceQuestion: Does Lean Deck protect host adapters, hardware calibration, requested explanation, and one-check minimums from false less-code pressure?
